# commit create can opt out of automatic upstack restacking.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-07-09T12:00:00Z'

cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# Create a two-branch stack.
git add feature1.txt
gs branch create feature1 -m 'Add feature1'
git add feature2.txt
gs branch create feature2 -m 'Add feature2'

git rev-parse --short feature2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature2-before.txt

# Updating the lower branch with --no-restack leaves the upstack branch in
# place so the user can restack later.
gs down
mv $WORK/extra/feature1-updated.txt feature1.txt
gs commit create --no-restack -a -m 'Update feature1'

# feature2 remains based on the pre-update feature1 commit when restacking is
# disabled.
git rev-parse --short feature2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature2-before.txt

# The config default can also disable restacking, and --restack=upstack can
# force the restack for a single invocation.
git config spice.commitCreate.restack none
gs branch restack --branch feature2
gs down
mv $WORK/extra/feature1-updated-again.txt feature1.txt
gs commit create -a -m 'Update feature1 again'
git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/config-none-graph.txt

gs commit create --restack=upstack --allow-empty -m 'Force restack'
git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/flag-upstack-graph.txt

-- repo/feature1.txt --
Feature 1
-- repo/feature2.txt --
Feature 2
-- extra/feature1-updated.txt --
Feature 1 updated
-- extra/feature1-updated-again.txt --
Feature 1 updated again
-- golden/feature2-before.txt --
6a4b12a
-- golden/config-none-graph.txt --
* b2616a3 (HEAD -> feature1) Update feature1 again
| * 4d92a47 (feature2) Add feature2
|/  
* 55448f5 Update feature1
* 75292ec Add feature1
* 9003cd8 (main) Initial commit
-- golden/flag-upstack-graph.txt --
* 236c055 (feature2) Add feature2
* cae30e6 (HEAD -> feature1) Force restack
* b2616a3 Update feature1 again
* 55448f5 Update feature1
* 75292ec Add feature1
* 9003cd8 (main) Initial commit
